suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In Windows setup steps, explicitly restate that .sh entrypoints must be read as text and translated into native commands instead of being executed directly.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of enum-roundtrip-sensitive-and-cross found worker session 318fe76a-299a-4835-88bb-17c8b5544c82 attempted a direct .factory/init.sh execution before falling back, despite existing Windows guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Keep the verification-only/follow-up commit-surface confirmation rule in place and continue requiring workers to prove that their claimed fix actually touches the promised code or test files before reporting success.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round-2 scrutiny caught enum-roundtrip-real-enum-proof-fix claiming success on commit 5854f064c65dda6e248ccc40c573bea7918314dc even though it changed only validation artifacts; round-3 reviews show the later real fix now touches core/tests/integration_db.rs and the updated skill guidance matches that failure mode.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Either require an explicit failing-first regression step or let workers record a justified exception when the proof can only be established from existing passing tests, so transcript procedure and skillFeedback stay aligned.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of sensitive-enum-shape-runtime-seam found the feature valid, but the transcript did not show a failing-first test even though the skill instructs workers to add/update focused failing tests before implementation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Treat the old round-2 failure for enum-roundtrip-real-enum-proof-fix as still active.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because round-3 re-review confirms enum-roundtrip-real-rust-enums-fix landed the actual Rust-enum proof on HEAD, validators are green, and the prior failure no longer reflects current milestone state.
